meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no version control (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no version control. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing & Version Control for Freelancers: A Guide
content.seo_description: Stop losing work. Master SaaS pricing models and implement version control strategies using Antigravity and Claude Code to scale your freelance business.
content.answer_block: To solve the 'no version control' crisis while managing SaaS pricing, integrate a Git-based workflow with automated pricing logic. By using Claude Code for repository state management and Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding, you ensure every pricing iteration is tracked, revertible, and ready for production deployment without manual errors.
content.intro:
You are staring at a production codebase that has no history, no branch protection, and a pricing model that is hard-coded into the UI. One wrong keystroke, and your client’s revenue stream evaporates. The anxiety of 'deploying in the dark' is the silent killer of freelance careers, turning simple updates into high-stakes gambles.

But it doesn't have to be this way. By treating your SaaS pricing logic as a version-controlled asset and leveraging AI-driven CLI tools, you transform your workflow from reactive firefighting into proactive engineering. This tutorial is your blueprint for stabilizing your stack and professionalizing your pricing infrastructure.

steps.title: Initialize Version Control for Pricing Logic
steps.description: Before touching pricing, you must isolate your business logic into a versioned repository to prevent accidental production overrides.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a modular directory structure for a SaaS pricing engine that separates business logic from UI components, ensuring all files are Git-ready.
steps.claude_code_command: git init && git add . && git commit -m 'Initial commit: pricing architecture'

steps.title: Abstract Pricing into a Config Schema
steps.description: Hard-coding prices is a rookie mistake. Move your pricing tiers into a versioned JSON schema to allow for rapid, traceable updates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a JSON schema for SaaS pricing tiers including features, monthly costs, and annual discount percentages.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Create a pricing.config.json file based on the schema and update the main application to ingest this file dynamically.'

steps.title: Implement Atomic Pricing Commits
steps.description: Every pricing change must be a distinct, documented commit. This allows you to revert to a previous 'safe' pricing state instantly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a pre-commit hook script that validates the pricing.config.json against the schema before allowing a commit.
steps.claude_code_command: git checkout -b feature/update-pricing-2024 && git commit -am 'Update: Adjust tier 2 pricing'

steps.title: Automate Pricing Validation Tests
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate unit tests for your pricing logic, ensuring that logic changes don't break existing revenue calculations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write Jest test cases to verify that pricing calculations handle edge cases like tax, discounts, and currency conversion correctly.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Generate unit tests for pricing logic and add them to the test suite.'

steps.title: Deploy via Version-Controlled CI/CD
steps.description: Link your repository to a deployment pipeline so that pricing updates are only pushed after successful code reviews.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs pricing validation tests on every pull request to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: git push origin feature/update-pricing-2024 && gh pr create --title 'Pricing Update' --body 'Updating pricing tiers for Q3.'

pro_tips: Always use Environment Variables for API keys related to payment gateways; never commit your keys, even if the repo is private.
Implement 'Feature Flags' for pricing updates so you can roll back a new pricing model for specific users without a full redeploy.
Use Git tags (e.g., v1.0.1-pricing) to mark specific pricing iterations, making it trivial to see exactly what prices were active on a specific date.
Keep a 'Change Log' inside your repo root; clients love seeing a transparent history of when and why prices were modified.
Automate your pricing documentation by having Claude Code update your README.md whenever the pricing.config.json file is modified.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing is versioned, integrate a dashboard that pulls data from your config file to visualize revenue projections in real-time.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start shipping with the confidence of a lead engineer. Our community of high-level AI builders is waiting to help you scale your freelance practice. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
